title: Orthogonal Contradiction (Two Potency Methods Disagree)
source: synthetic (SP v5 Section 8.2.1 tricky scenario: binding assay shows comparable but cell-based potency shows a difference, creating analytical contradiction)
scenario_description: FcRn binding assay (3 methods, 10 lots) shows the product is comparable at 99% relative potency. However, the cell-based bioassay (1 method, 4 lots) shows 85% relative potency with high variability (CV 15%). The contradiction between two potency methods that should correlate creates an evidence gap requiring investigation. The system should flag 'Insufficient Evidence' because a CQA (Potency) has high residual uncertainty (>0.6) even though no major/critical concerns are flagged.
change_description: CHO cell culture temperature shift from 37°C to 35°C to improve yield. FcRn binding assay (SPR, 3 methods, 10 lots) shows comparable binding at 99% relative activity. Cell-based potency assay (1 method, 4 lots) shows 85% relative potency with high CV. The two methods that should agree on functional activity give contradictory results, requiring investigation to determine which better reflects in vivo potency.
_verdict_note: JC verdict is proceed (Comparable) with consistency warning about CQA uncertainty. Previously overridden to Insufficient Evidence by legacy guard. Known gap: JC does not yet detect contradictory functional assay results.
